name: Brewster Kahle
org: Internet Archive (founder); Thinking Machines; WAIS Inc.
why_notable: Founded the Internet Archive (1996) with the mission of universal access to all knowledge. Built the Wayback Machine — which stores 800+ billion archived web pages — and the CDX (Capture Index) API that tools like waybackurls query to retrieve historical URLs. Without the Internet Archive, a major class of passive recon disappears entirely: no historical URL enumeration, no dead-endpoint surfacing, no parameter archaeology. Also created WAIS, a precursor to web search. One of the few people to build a lasting public-good internet institution.
